answers.body:
Maybe this answer is a little bit late to the party, but nevertheless. When I am dealing with $filter Option I: Traverse the provided AST (Abstract Syntax Tree), using pre-order (depth-first).

The following method processes the Expression recursively.

    private Predicate<Product> processExpression(Expression expression) {
        if (expression instanceof Binary) {
            return handleBinaryExpression((Binary) expression);
        } else if (expression instanceof Method) {
            return handleMethodExpression((Method) expression);
        } else if (expression instanceof Unary) {
            return handleNotExpression((Unary) expression);
        }
        throw new ODataRuntimeException("Unsupported expression type: " + expression.getClass());
    }

If the Expression is an instance of Method The following function is being called:

    private Predicate<Product> handleMethodExpression(Method expression) {
        MethodKind metOperator = expression.getMethod();

        if (metOperator == MethodKind.STARTSWITH){
            return handleStartsWith(expression);
        }
        else if (metOperator == MethodKind.ENDSWITH){
            return handleEndsWith(expression);
        }
        else if (metOperator == MethodKind.CONTAINS){
            return handleContains(expression);
        }

        throw new ODataRuntimeException("Unsupported method type: " + metOperator);
    }

In your case, the metKind is of type MethodKind.CONTAINS and the following function is called:

    private Predicate<Product> handleContains(Method expression) {
        Member member = (Member) expression.getParameters().get(0);
        Literal literal = (Literal) expression.getParameters().get(1);


        // Field searching
        String fieldName = FilterOptionHelper.extractFieldNameFromMember((Member) member);
        Metadata<Product, ?> metadata = getMetadata(fieldName);

        // Field validating
        Class<?> type = metadata.javaType();
        if (!type.equals(String.class)) {
            throw new ODataRuntimeException("Field type is not String " + fieldName);
        }

        String value = literal.getText();
        if (value != null && value.length() >= 2 && value.startsWith("'") && value.endsWith("'")) {
            value = value.substring(1, value.length() - 1);
        }

        // Use the existing typed getter
        Function<Product, ?> rawGetter = metadata.getter();
        @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
        Function<Product, String> stringGetter =
                (Function<Product, String>) (Function<?, ?>) rawGetter;

        // Return a dynamic predicate
        String finalValue = value;
        return entity -> {
            String fieldValue = stringGetter.apply(entity);
            return fieldValue != null && fieldValue.contains(finalValue);
        };
    }

This is how can a dynamic Predicate can be constructed, at least how I do it.

p.s This is how extractFieldNameFromMember method looks in my implementation.

    public static String extractFieldNameFromMember(Member member) {
        List<UriResource> uriResources = member.getResourcePath().getUriResourceParts();

        UriResourcePrimitiveProperty property = (UriResourcePrimitiveProperty) uriResources.get(0);

        return property.getProperty().getName();
    }

There are limitation and maybe the edge cases but for your endpoint with $filter=contains(name,'myProductName') it should work.

p.s # 2

My Metadata class looks like this:

public class Metadata<T, R> {
    private final String edmName;                  // OData property name
    private final Class<R> javaType;               // normalized Java type you return to the visitor
    private final Function<T, R> getter;           // how to read the value from the entity
    private final String jpaPath;                  // optional: for DB pushdown later (e.g., "status" or "assignee.name")

    private Metadata(String edmName, Class<R> javaType, Function<T, R> getter, String jpaPath) {
        this.edmName = edmName;
        this.javaType = javaType;
        this.getter = getter;
        this.jpaPath = jpaPath;
    }

    public static <T,R> Metadata<T,R> of(String edmName, Class<R> type, Function<T,R> getter) {
        return new Metadata<>(edmName, type, getter, null);
    }
    public static <T,R> Metadata<T,R> of(String edmName, Class<R> type, Function<T,R> getter, String jpaPath) {
        return new Metadata<>(edmName, type, getter, jpaPath);
    }

    public String edmName()  { return edmName; }
    public Class<R> javaType(){ return javaType; }
    public Function<T,R> getter(){ return getter; }
    public String jpaPath()  { return jpaPath; }
}

The MetadataProvider<T> interface:

public interface MetadataProvider<T> {
    Map<String, Metadata<T, ?>> getAllMetadata();
    Metadata<T, ?> getMetadata(String edmName);
}

And a concrete implementation example for your case:

public class ProductPropertyDescriptor implements MetadataProvider<Product> {

    private static final Map<String, Metadata<Product, ?>> META = Map.of(
        "Id",         Metadata.of("Id", Integer.class,     Product::getId,        "id"),
        "Name",       Metadata.of("Name", String.class,    Product::getName,      "name"),
        "Sku",        Metadata.of("Sku", String.class,     Product::getSku,       "sku"),
        "Price",      Metadata.of("Price", BigDecimal.class, Product::getPrice,   "price"),
        "Active",     Metadata.of("Active", Boolean.class, Product::getActive,    "active"),
        "CreatedAt",  Metadata.of("CreatedAt", Instant.class, Product::getCreatedAt, "created_at"),
        "UpdatedAt",  Metadata.of("UpdatedAt", Instant.class, Product::getUpdatedAt, "updated_at")
    );

    @Override
    public Map<String, Metadata<Product, ?>> getAllMetadata() {
        return META;
    }

    @Override
    public Metadata<Product, ?> getMetadata(String edmName) {
        return META.get(edmName);
    }
}

title: Implement $Filter contains() using Olingo odata in java
body:
I'm trying to utilize olingo odata 4 library to implement $filter and specifically contains() logic. We have already implemented our rest APIs with the database. So we don't want to use any other features that olingo odata4 library provides.

for example below is my endpoint https:localhost:8095/myapi/Products?$filter=contains(name,'myProductName')

$filter is the query parameter and I can fetch contains(name,'myProductName') from that.

All I need to do is verify that it is contains() function and returns a Map with Key = name and Value = myProductName

I am stuck at UriInfo part, I don't have edm or odata.

We might add more filter functions that's why our requirement is to use olingo odata4 library.

I looked at so many documentations and there are no tutorials that I found useful for my requirement.

Please let me know if there are good documentations that I can follow or any further discussions will be helpful.

Thanks in advance!
